nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# Amazon Product Price Tracker

This workflow automates price monitoring for Amazon products using the ScrapeOps API. It tracks price changes over time, alerts you when prices cross your defined thresholds, and maintains a historical record of all price movements.

## Features
- Scheduled price checks for multiple Amazon products
- Price change calculations (absolute and percentage)
- Smart alerting based on customizable thresholds
- Automated email notifications with detailed price information
- Historical price tracking for trend analysis

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## API Configuration
This workflow requires a ScrapeOps API key to fetch Amazon product data.
To obtain your API key, register at {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## API Documentation
For detailed information about the Amazon Product API endpoint used in this workflow,
refer to the official documentation at:
{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
The documentation provides details on all available parameters, response formats,
and best practices for optimizing your API usage.

## Integration Setup
Once registered, insert your API key in the "Scrapeops - Amazon Product" node parameters.
This workflow uses the structured data endpoint which returns clean, parsed product data
in a consistent JSON format.

## Google Sheets Configuration
A Google Sheets spreadsheet is used to store the product data collected through this workflow.
The original template spreadsheet is shared in read-only mode through this link:
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

To use this workflow:
1. Access the shared spreadsheet using the link above
2. Make your own copy by going to File > Make a copy
3. Share your copy with appropriate permissions
4. In the n8n workflow, locate the "Setup" node
5. Update the "spreadsheet_url" variable with the link to YOUR copy of the spreadsheet

This ensures each user works with their own separate spreadsheet, avoiding data overlap 
between different users while maintaining the original structure needed by the workflow.

parameters.content:
# Amazon Product Price Tracker

This workflow automates price monitoring for Amazon products using the ScrapeOps API. It tracks price changes over time, alerts you when prices cross your defined thresholds, and maintains a historical record of all price movements.

## Features
- Scheduled price checks for multiple Amazon products
- Price change calculations (absolute and percentage)
- Smart alerting based on customizable thresholds
- Automated email notifications with detailed price information
- Historical price tracking for trend analysis

## API Configuration
This workflow requires a ScrapeOps API key to fetch Amazon product data.
To obtain your API key, register at {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## API Documentation
For detailed information about the Amazon Product API endpoint used in this workflow,
refer to the official documentation at:
{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
The documentation provides details on all available parameters, response formats,
and best practices for optimizing your API usage.

## Integration Setup
Once registered, insert your API key in the "Scrapeops - Amazon Product" node parameters.
This workflow uses the structured data endpoint which returns clean, parsed product data
in a consistent JSON format.

## Google Sheets Configuration
A Google Sheets spreadsheet is used to store the product data collected through this workflow.
The original template spreadsheet is shared in read-only mode through this link:
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

To use this workflow:
1. Access the shared spreadsheet using the link above
2. Make your own copy by going to File > Make a copy
3. Share your copy with appropriate permissions
4. In the n8n workflow, locate the "Setup" node
5. Update the "spreadsheet_url" variable with the link to YOUR copy of the spreadsheet

This ensures each user works with their own separate spreadsheet, avoiding data overlap 
between different users while maintaining the original structure needed by the workflow.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Products to Monitor" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Scrapeops - Amazon Product" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Insert - Price History" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Update - Products to Monitor" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Send Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.emailSend) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Check Valid Price" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Alert Decision" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Last Price".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Price Change".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Alert Status".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Setup".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
